response_raw:
{
"mode": "human",
"topic": "How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.",
"opinion": "As an elderly woman who has been using social media for a few years, I have noticed that there is a lot of fake content on these platforms. From fake news to fake profiles, it can be overwhelming and difficult to know what to believe. However, there are a few things you can do to manage and deal with fake content on social media. Firstly, it's important to fact-check everything you see on social media. Don't just take information at face value, go to reputable sources to verify the accuracy of the information. Secondly, be cautious when engaging with profiles that seem too good to be true or have suspiciously high follower counts. Legitimate profiles will have a more natural flow of interactions and won't be overly aggressive in their messaging. Thirdly, use privacy settings to control who can interact with you on social media. This will help prevent unwanted interactions and limit the amount of fake content that you see. Finally, don't be afraid to report suspicious profiles or content to the platform's moderators. Most social media platforms have systems in place to detect and remove fake content, so by reporting it, you can help keep the platform safe and secure for everyone."
,"reasoning_summary": "To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to fact-check everything, be cautious when engaging with suspicious profiles, use privacy settings to control interactions, and report suspicious content to the platform's moderators. By taking these steps, you can help keep your social media experience safe and secure."
,"tone": "educated",

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As an elderly woman who has been using social media for a few years, I have noticed that there is a lot of fake content on these platforms. From fake news to fake profiles, it can be overwhelming and difficult to know what to believe. However, there are a few things you can do to manage and deal with fake content on social media. Firstly, it's important to fact-check everything you see on social media. Don't just take information at face value, go to reputable sources to verify the accuracy of the information. Secondly, be cautious when engaging with profiles that seem too good to be true or have suspiciously high follower counts. Legitimate profiles will have a more natural flow of interactions and won't be overly aggressive in their messaging. Thirdly, use privacy settings to control who can interact with you on social media. This will help prevent unwanted interactions and limit the amount of fake content that you see. Finally, don't be afraid to report suspicious profiles or content to the platform's moderators. Most social media platforms have systems in place to detect and remove fake content, so by reporting it, you can help keep the platform safe and secure for everyone.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: To manage and deal with fake content on social media, it is important to fact-check everything, be cautious when engaging with suspicious profiles, use privacy settings to control interactions, and report suspicious content to the platform's moderators. By taking these steps, you can help keep your social media experience safe and secure.
